title: Prices, Colors & Sizes
description: Learn to ask about prices, use color adjectives with correct gender endings, describe sizes, and use demonstratives (ten/tato/to) in shopping contexts.

exercises.prompt: What does 'Kolik to stojí?' mean in English?

data.explanation: Kolik to stojí? = How much does it cost? This is the essential phrase for asking prices in Czech shops.

exercises.prompt: Which color adjective correctly agrees with the feminine noun 'košile' (shirt)?

data.question_cz: Které přídavné jméno správně souhlasí se slovem 'košile'?
data.question_en: Which adjective correctly agrees with 'košile' (shirt, feminine)?
data.explanation: košile is feminine, so the adjective must end in -á: červená košile. -ý is masculine, -é is neuter.

exercises.prompt: Translate 'How much does this cost?' to Czech.

data.grammar_note: Kolik = how much, to = it/this, stojí = costs (3rd person sg of 'stát'). Use this phrase in any shop.

exercises.prompt: Complete with the correct color adjective (modrý) agreeing with the noun.

data.sentence: Ten ___ (modrý) svetr je hezký, ale ta ___ (modrý) sukně je lepší.
data.explanation: svetr is masculine → modrý. sukně is feminine → modrá. Colors change endings based on the gender of the noun they describe.

exercises.prompt: Which demonstrative pronoun is correct for 'kniha' (book, feminine)?

data.question_cz: Které ukazovací zájmeno je správné pro 'kniha'?
data.question_en: Which demonstrative is correct for 'kniha' (book, feminine)?
data.explanation: kniha is feminine → ta kniha. ten = masculine, to = neuter, ty = plural. Demonstratives must match the gender of the noun.

exercises.prompt: Translate 'That is a small shirt' to Czech.

data.grammar_note: To je = That is. malá = small (feminine ending, matching košile). košile = shirt. Demonstratives and adjectives share gender agreement.

exercises.prompt: Complete with the correct size adjectives.

data.sentence: Ten svetr je ___ (big), ale ta bunda je ___ (small).
data.explanation: svetr is masculine → velký. bunda is feminine → malá. Size adjectives follow the same gender agreement rules as colors.

exercises.prompt: Listen and write the Czech price question.

data.note: This is the essential shopping phrase — 'How much does it cost?'

exercises.prompt: Say this phrase aloud describing a shirt.
Translate 'I want that red dress' to Czech.

data.grammar_note: chci = I want. ty = those (plural, šaty is plural-only). červené = red (plural adjective ending). šaty = dress.

exercises.prompt: Complete the shopping dialogue about prices.

data.scenario: You are in a Czech shop and want to know the price of a sweater.

lines.speaker: person_a
lines.text: Ten svetr stojí pět set korun.

accepted_answers: Ano, kolik stojí ten svetr?|Ano, kolik stojí ten svetr
Dobře, vezmu si ho. Děkuju.|Dobře, vezmu si ho. Děkuju.|Dobře, vezmu si ho|Děkuju, vezmu si ho
data.explanation: 'Dobrý den, můžu vám pomoct?' = 'Hello, can I help you?' — 'Kolik stojí ten svetr?' = 'How much does the sweater cost?' — 'Stojí pět set korun' = 'It costs 500 crowns.' — 'Vezmu si ho' = 'I'll take it.'

exercises.answer_key: Ano, kolik stojí ten svetr?|Dobře, vezmu si ho. Děkuju.

data.explanation: střední = medium (size). malý = small, velký = big. Unlike malý/velký, 'střední' has the same form for all genders.
